title: Gated v4 tools (fleet_verify + parity-gated harness_activate) live in framework dev tree but NOT yet in the master wheel
situation: S45 built tools/fleet_verify.py (AC3) and folded the parity gate into tools/harness_activate.py (AC1), tested (14 AP tests) with the full suite green (488). The normal flow is to canonicalize these into the master wheel (mywheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/managed/tools) and recut the master MANIFEST. The in-place master write was correctly boundary-blocked by the harness (master is outside this repo + cross-tree write = gated step, not a silent copy).
decision: Stopped at the boundary rather than working around it. The work is durable in the framework git repo; canonicalization-to-master is deferred to an explicitly human-authorized step.
consequence: Until canonicalization happens: (a) the master wheel still carries the UNGATED harness_activate.py — a forced/automated activation that reads the master copy would not enforce the parity gate; (b) fleet_verify.py is absent from the master, so spokes cannot self-verify from a distributed copy; (c) drift persists between framework/tools (gated, has fleet_verify, lacks harness_upgrade) and master managed/tools (ungated activate, has harness_upgrade).
mitigation: v4 is DORMANT fleet-wide (fleet_verify confirms 0/26 active), so no live path currently reads the ungated master copy. The risk is latent, not active.
clear_when: Human authorizes the master write; then: cp framework/tools/{fleet_verify.py,harness_activate.py} into mywheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/managed/tools, recut master MANIFEST (manifest_build.py), verify, reconcile harness_upgrade.py drift. Tracked in task-fleet-recurrency-before-activation-v1 (AC2 / notes).
resolution: User authorized the master write (S45). Canonicalized fleet_verify.py + parity-gated harness_activate.py + cruft-excluding manifest_build.py into mywheel/WAI-Harness/spoke/managed/tools; master MANIFEST recut to 154 files, verify ok (0/0/0). Discovered+fixed a manifest_build bug (was baking 11 __pycache__/.pyc files into the manifest); master now self-verifies stably. Drift note: master had harness_upgrade.py while framework dev tree did not — reconcile direction is framework<-master for that file (still open, low risk).
